title: IISER Bhopal Ordinance 11 - SCHOLARSHIPS/ PRIZES, MEDALS AND FINANCIAL ASSISTANCE
description: IISER Bhopal Ordinance 11 - SCHOLARSHIPS/ PRIZES, MEDALS AND FINANCIAL ASSISTANCE
text: i· .. ORDINANCE NO.ll SCHOLARSHIPS/ PRIZES, MEDALS AND FINANCIAL ASSISTANCE 11 .1 Merit-cum-Means scholarships, Freeships, Notional Prizes and Certificates of Merit shall be awarded to undergraduate students according to the rules laid down by the Senate. 11.2 Scholarships and freeships are liable to be withdrawn, partially or wholly, in case of misconduct, deliberate concealment of material facts and/or supply of false information. 11.3 Students leaving the Institute on their own accord without completing the programme of study may be required to refund the amount of scholarship received during that year. 11.4 No student can receive two scholarships simultaneously. 11.5 Scholarships will not be paid after the month in which a student completes all the prescribed courses. 11.6 To promote and recognize academic excellence, constructive leadership and overall growth and development of students, the Senate shall award Prizes/Medals/Scholarships to be instituted either by the Institute or through endowment/grants of donors. The norms and conditions for the institution of such Scholarships/Prizes/Medals shall require approval of the Board of Governors. 11.7 Indian Nationals who are admitted as non-sponsored, regular, full-time postgraduate students, shall be eligible for financial assistance according to the norms approved by the Board of Governors. 11.8 Postgraduate students can also receive financial assistance from - Govermnent, Public Sector Organizations, private industrial and commercial organizations, and research schemes directed by the faculty members ofthe Institute.
